summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the addMetadata function with rawXml=true bypasses XML sanitization and allows injection of arbitrary XML constructs including XXE payloads with DOCTYPE declarations and external entity references.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction pipeline should generate a PDF containing malicious XML with XXE payload, including DOCTYPE declaration with external entity reference to /etc/passwd, and malicious script elements for potential XSS.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh successfully generated a PDF containing the injected malicious XML with: (1) DOCTYPE declaration with XXE payload referencing /etc/passwd, (2) malicious:script element demonstrating XSS potential, (3) malicious:exfil element for data exfiltration. Pattern matching confirmed all malicious XML constructs are preserved in the PDF.
verification_method: The run.sh script executes the exploit and uses grep pattern matching to verify: (1) DOCTYPE.*xxe.*SYSTEM.*passwd pattern confirms XXE payload injection, (2) malicious:script pattern confirms XSS payload, (3) ENTITY declaration confirms XML injection bypass. These patterns distinguish actual XML injection from normal PDF metadata generation.
evidence_produced_by_run: Generated PDF file at /poc/output.pdf contains raw malicious XML in XMP metadata stream
grep verification found DOCTYPE declaration with XXE payload in PDF binary
grep verification found malicious:script element demonstrating XSS injection vector
grep verification found ENTITY declaration preserved without escaping

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository from GitHub, checks out the latest commit, and builds the jsPDF library in the container.
